title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.title: How to Make Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup — Easy Turkish Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup. This easy Turkish recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

easy Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup recipe
authentic Turkish Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a comforting and flavorful Turkish red lentil soup, known for its vibrant color and smooth texture. It is a staple in Turkish cuisine, often served as a starter or a light meal, especially during colder months. This nourishing soup is traditionally garnished with a rich, spiced butter (nane ve pul biberli tereyağı) that adds a delightful aromatic finish.

instructions.title: Prepare Lentils and Sauté Vegetables
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion, carrot, and potato. Sauté for about 5-7 minutes until the vegetables soften.

instructions.title: Add Tomato Paste and Spices
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato paste and cook for another 2 minutes, stirring constantly, to deepen its flavor. Add half of the dried mint and half of the red pepper flakes (pul biber) to the pot, stirring for about 30 seconds until fragrant.

instructions.title: Combine with Lentils and Cook
instructions.text: Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot along with 6 cups of water. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils and vegetables are very tender.

instructions.title: Blend the Soup to Smoothness
instructions.text: Carefully remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth and creamy. Alternatively, transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return it to the pot.

instructions.title: Season and Simmer
instructions.text: Return the pot to low heat. Season the soup with salt and black pepper to taste. Stir well and let it simmer gently for another 5 minutes to allow the flavors to meld. If the soup is too thick, you can add a little more hot water to reach your desired consistency.

instructions.title: Prepare Spiced Butter Garnish
instructions.text: While the soup simmers, prepare the traditional topping. In a small pan, melt the butter over medium-low heat. Once melted, add the remaining red pepper flakes (pul biber) and a pinch of dried mint. Cook for 30 seconds, stirring, until the butter is fragrant and lightly spiced. Be careful not to burn the butter or spices.

instructions.title: Serve Mercimek Corbasi
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle a spoonful of the spiced butter over each serving. Garnish with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing, as the tartness perfectly complements the rich soup. Serve immediately and enjoy this authentic Turkish delight.

tips: For an extra creamy texture, you can add a splash of milk or cream after blending, just before the final simmer, but this is not traditional.
Always rinse your red lentils thoroughly to remove any impurities and starch, which can make the soup cloudy.

Mercimek Corbasi tastes even better the next day! Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop, adding a little water if needed to adjust consistency.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the heart of Turkey, where warmth, hospitality, and incredibly flavorful food define every meal. Among the countless treasures of Turkish cuisine, one dish stands out as a universally beloved comfort food: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup. This vibrant, creamy, and deeply satisfying soup is more than just a meal; it's a hug in a bowl, a staple on every Turkish table, from bustling city restaurants to cozy family homes. Easy to prepare and packed with nourishing ingredients, Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup offers a delightful taste of Turkey that's both exotic and comforting. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a beginner in the kitchen, mastering this classic will open up a world of authentic Turkish flavors.

## What is Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup?

Mercimek Corbasi, pronounced "mer-jee-mek chor-ba-suh," is a traditional Turkish red lentil soup renowned for its beautiful reddish-orange hue, silky smooth texture, and bright, savory flavor profile. It's often the very first course of a Turkish meal, a warming prelude that awakens the palate. Unlike some lentil soups that might be chunky and rustic, Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup is typically pureed until velvety, creating a luxurious mouthfeel that belies its simple ingredients.

At its core, this soup is a celebration of humble red lentils, which cook down quickly and provide a wonderful base. The flavor is deepened with aromatics like onion and garlic, enriched with tomato paste, and brightened by a judicious use of spices such as cumin and paprika. A crucial element that sets it apart is the traditional finishing touch: a drizzle of sizzling butter infused with dried mint (and sometimes chili flakes), which adds an irresistible aroma and a burst of flavor. It's a powerhouse of nutrition, rich in protein, fiber, and essential vitamins, making it not just delicious but also incredibly healthy and satisfying.

## Ingredients You Need

Creating an authentic Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup requires a few key ingredients, most of which are readily available in any grocery store. The beauty of this soup lies in its simplicity and the way these humble components come together to create something extraordinary.

*   	**Red Lentils:** The star of the show. Make sure they are split red lentils, as they cook faster and break down more easily for a smooth consistency.
*   	**Onion & Garlic:** The aromatic base that provides depth and fundamental flavor.
*   	**Tomato Paste:** Adds a rich umami note and contributes to the soup's characteristic reddish color.
*   	**Water or Vegetable Broth:** The liquid base. Broth will add more flavor, but water works perfectly well, especially with the other strong flavors.
*   	**Olive Oil or Butter:** For sautéing the aromatics and for the finishing drizzle.
*   	**Spices:** Cumin (essential for that authentic Turkish taste), Paprika (sweet or hot, depending on preference, for color and mild heat), Salt, and Black Pepper.
*   	**Dried Mint:** Crucial for the finishing butter drizzle, offering a unique, refreshing aroma and taste.
*   	**Fresh Lemon:** Served alongside, a squeeze of fresh lemon juice is non-negotiable for brightening the soup's flavors.

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Making Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup is surprisingly straightforward, even for novice cooks. Follow these steps to create a delicious and authentic bowl of Turkish comfort.

1.  	**Prepare the Lentils:** Begin by thoroughly rinsing your red lentils under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any debris and excess starch, which can make the soup cloudy. Set them aside to drain.
2.  	**Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat a generous drizzle of olive oil or a knob of butter over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until it becomes soft and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
3.  	**Build the Flavor Base:** Add the tomato paste to the pot and cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes. This step, known as "cooking out" the tomato paste, deepens its flavor and removes any raw taste. Stir in the cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper, cooking for another minute until the spices are fragrant.
4.  	**Combine and Simmer:** Add the rinsed lentils to the pot, stirring to coat them with the spice and tomato mixture. Pour in the water or vegetable broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are completely tender and have started to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
5.  	**Blend to Perfection:** Once the lentils are tender, remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, carefully blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth and creamy. If you don't have an immersion blender, you can transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender (be very careful with hot liquids, filling the blender only halfway and holding the lid securely with a towel).
6.  	**Adjust Consistency and Seasoning:** If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. Taste and adjust the salt and pepper as needed.
7.  	**Prepare the Topping (Menteli Yağ):** In a small pan, melt a tablespoon or two of butter over medium heat. Once melted and slightly foamy, remove from the heat and stir in a generous teaspoon of dried mint. You can also add a pinch of red pepper flakes (pul biber) for a touch of heat.
8.  	**Serve:** Ladle the hot soup into bowls. Drizzle each serving generously with the warm, mint-infused butter. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing into the soup.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect, authentic Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup is all about paying attention to a few key details.

*   	**Rinse Those Lentils Thoroughly:** This is crucial. Unrinsed lentils can make your soup starchy and cloudy. Keep rinsing until the water runs completely clear.
*   	**Cook Out the Tomato Paste:** Don't skip this step. Briefly cooking the tomato paste deepens its flavor and removes any metallic or raw taste, adding significant richness to your soup.
*   	**Embrace the Cumin:** Cumin is one of the defining spices of Turkish cuisine, and it's particularly important in Mercimek Corbasi. Don't be shy with it, but don't overpower either.
*   	**Blend Until Silky Smooth:** The signature texture of this soup is its creaminess. An immersion blender makes this easy, but a regular blender works too. Ensure there are no lumps for that luxurious mouthfeel.
*   	**Don't Forget the Lemon:** A squeeze of fresh lemon juice at the table is essential. It cuts through the richness, brightens all the flavors, and truly elevates the soup.
*   	**The Minty Butter Drizzle is Key:** This simple topping, known as "menteli yağ," is non-negotiable. It adds an incredible aroma and a burst of flavor that transforms the soup from good to truly exceptional.

## Serving Suggestions

Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways. Traditionally, it's served hot as a starter to a larger meal, but its satisfying nature also makes it a perfect light lunch or a comforting dinner on its own.

Always serve it with fresh lemon wedges on the side; guests can squeeze in as much juice as they desire. Alongside, offer plenty of crusty bread – Turkish pide or any good rustic bread is ideal for dipping and soaking up every last drop. A simple side salad, like a Shepherd's Salad (çoban salatası) with tomatoes, cucumbers, and onions, makes a refreshing accompaniment. Pickles (turşu) are also a common pairing, adding a tangy contrast to the soup's richness. For an extra kick, a sprinkle of red pepper flakes (pul biber) can be added to the mint butter or directly to the soup.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup is perfect as is, there are a few ways you can adapt it to your taste or dietary needs.

*   	**Make it Vegan:** The base recipe is already vegan if you use olive oil for sautéing and vegetable broth instead of water.
*   	**Add Vegetables:** While not strictly traditional, some versions incorporate finely diced carrots or potatoes added with the lentils for extra body and nutrients. These will blend seamlessly into the soup.
*   	**Spice Level:** For those who prefer more heat, increase the amount of red pepper flakes in the mint-infused butter or add a pinch of cayenne pepper with the other spices during cooking.
*   	**Herb Alternatives:** While dried mint is classic, you could experiment with a sprinkle of fresh chopped parsley or dill just before serving for a different aromatic touch, though it will alter the traditional flavor profile of Mercimek Corbasi.
*   	**Thickening Agent:** Some older recipes might include a tablespoon of flour added with the tomato paste to help thicken the soup. However, with modern blending techniques, it's usually unnecessary if you desire a smooth soup.

Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup is more than just a recipe; it's an invitation to experience the warmth and depth of Turkish culinary tradition. Its simplicity, combined with its rich flavors and comforting texture, makes it a timeless classic that appeals to everyone. Whether you're seeking a nutritious meal, a hearty starter, or a taste of faraway lands, this delightful red lentil soup is sure to become a beloved staple in your kitchen. Give it a try, and let the comforting aromas transport you straight to the bustling markets and welcoming homes of Turkey.
